label: Gaza City
sub: Major population center; heavily bombarded in Oct 2023 – Mar 2025 IDF operations. Pre-war population ~700,000.

label: Rafah
sub: Southern Gaza city at Egyptian border; IDF major ground operation began May 2024. ~1.4M displaced persons sheltering at peak.

label: Khan Yunis
sub: Second-largest Gaza city; major IDF ground operation Dec 2023 – Apr 2024. Yahya Sinwar's home city.

label: Jabaliya Refugee Camp
sub: Largest refugee camp in Gaza (pop. ~110,000). Site of First Intifada outbreak 1987. Repeatedly struck by IDF 2023-2025.

label: Al-Shifa Hospital
sub: Gaza's largest hospital. IDF raided Nov 2023 and Mar 2024 claiming Hamas command infrastructure. WHO reported hospital rendered non-functional.

label: Kerem Shalom / Karem Abu Salem
sub: Main commercial crossing between Israel and Gaza for goods. Repeatedly closed during military operations; targeted by Hamas mortar fire.

label: Rafah Border Crossing
sub: Only Gaza crossing not controlled by Israel; connects to Egypt. Closed by Israel after Oct 7 seizure of Philadelphi Corridor. Sole evacuation route for many civilians.

label: Erez / Beit Hanoun Crossing
sub: Northern Gaza pedestrian crossing. Attacked and destroyed by Hamas on Oct 7, 2023. Basis for proposed aid port in northern Gaza.

label: Kibbutz Kfar Aza
sub: One of the hardest-hit Israeli communities on Oct 7; 64 residents killed, many homes destroyed. Remains uninhabited as of 2026.

label: Kibbutz Be'eri
sub: 108 residents killed on Oct 7 — deadliest Oct 7 site for a single community. Hamas also murdered hostages during IDF response.

label: Nova Music Festival Site
sub: 364 attendees massacred by Hamas gunmen on Oct 7. Site near Kibbutz Re'im. Memorial erected; site visited by world leaders.

label: Jerusalem — Old City
sub: Contains Temple Mount/Haram al-Sharif (holy to Jews and Muslims), Western Wall, Church of Holy Sepulchre. Sovereignty disputed; both Israel and Palestine claim capital status.

label: Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif
sub: Most contested religious site. Israeli visits by Likud members (Sharon 2000, Ben-Gvir 2023+) have triggered crises. Status quo arrangement administered by Jordanian Waqf. Ben-Gvir stormed the compound on April 12 (third incursion in 2026). On April 22 (Israel's 78th Independence Day), 526 settlers stormed the compound under police escort, raising Israeli flags and performing rituals — one of the largest single-day incursions of 2026. Pakistan formally condemned the act as a 'blatant violation of international law.'

label: Ramallah — PA Headquarters
sub: De facto administrative capital of the Palestinian Authority. Muqata'a compound — PA Presidential offices, Arafat's tomb. In Area A (full PA control).

label: Hebron (Al-Khalil) — H2
sub: Divided city: H1 (PA) and H2 (Israeli military control, ~500 settlers among 30,000 Palestinians). Most contested West Bank flashpoint. Cave of the Patriarchs / Ibrahimi Mosque.

label: Jenin Refugee Camp
sub: Northern West Bank refugee camp; IDF Operation Defensive Shield 2002 killed dozens. Continued IDF counterterrorism raids 2023-2026. Stronghold of armed Palestinian factions.

label: Nablus
sub: Major West Bank city; frequent site of IDF raids. Home of Al-Aqsa Martyrs' Brigades, Lion's Den faction. Rising West Bank violence 2022-2025.

label: Ma'ale Adumim
sub: Largest Israeli settlement in the West Bank (~40,000 residents). Located just east of Jerusalem, effectively bisects Palestinian West Bank. Subject of US warnings against annexation.

label: Ariel
sub: Major settlement in central West Bank (~20,000 residents), 20km east of Green Line. Location is a key obstacle to territorial contiguity in two-state proposals.

label: Gush Etzion
sub: Settlement bloc south of Jerusalem (~80,000 residents across multiple settlements). Jewish presence pre-1948; often cited in Israeli positions as core settlement area.

label: Qalandia Checkpoint
sub: Main checkpoint between Ramallah and Jerusalem. Symbol of West Bank movement restrictions; 20,000+ Palestinians cross daily for work. Site of frequent confrontations.

label: Bethlehem
sub: Birthplace of Jesus; Palestinian city in Area A. Church of the Nativity. Separation barrier surrounds city on three sides, cutting it from Jerusalem. Economy dependent on Christian pilgrimage tourism.

label: Jericho
sub: Oldest continuously inhabited city in the world. First Palestinian city transferred to PA control under Oslo. In Area A; surrounded by Area C.

label: Deir el-Balah
sub: Central Gaza city; major UNRWA facilities. Became one of the largest displacement centers during 2023-2025 war. UN World Food Programme distribution hub.

label: Sheikh Jarrah
sub: East Jerusalem neighborhood; Israeli court-ordered evictions of Palestinian families sparked the May 2021 crisis and Operation Guardian of the Walls. Focal point of settler land claims.

label: Sderot
sub: Israeli city 1.5km from Gaza border; under constant rocket fire since 2001. Thousands of rockets have struck or landed near the city. Population partially evacuated Oct 2023.

label: Ashkelon
sub: Israeli coastal city; major target of Hamas rockets including on Oct 7. Contains power plant that historically supplied Gaza. Population ~155,000.

label: UNRWA Gaza Operations
sub: UNRWA operates 279 schools, 22 health centers in Gaza. HQ area in Gaza City damaged. Over 200 UNRWA staff killed since Oct 7. Israel accused UNRWA of Hamas ties; US suspended then partially restored funding.

label: Tulkarm
sub: Northern West Bank city; Nur Shams and Tulkarm refugee camps. Major IDF counterterrorism operations 2023-2025; site of sustained militant activity.

label: Beit Lahiya / Beit Hanoun
sub: Northern Gaza towns; among most heavily bombarded areas in 2023 war. Virtually entirely destroyed; remained under IDF control through 2025.

label: Haifa
sub: Israel's third-largest city; major port and technology hub. Struck by Hezbollah rockets during Lebanon conflict parallel to Gaza war (2024).

label: Philadelphi Corridor
sub: Narrow strip along Gaza-Egypt border; key smuggling route for Hamas weapons. Israeli military occupation of corridor was major ceasefire sticking point. IDF says tunnels ran beneath.

label: Jalud & Qaryut Villages
sub: Palestinian villages south of Nablus. Attacked by ~100 masked settlers March 22, 2026 (10+ homes torched, village council burned). Again on April 28, 2026, settlers burned a Palestinian family home and beat an elderly man while IDF soldiers stood by; 12 settlers involved, only 1 arrested. Repeated targeting makes Jalud a focal point of West Bank settler violence in 2026.

label: Silwan — Batn al-Hawa
sub: East Jerusalem neighborhood where 11 Palestinian families were forcibly evicted March 25, 2026 via Ateret Cohanim settler organization. ~90 families (700 people) at risk. Courts apply 1970 Israeli law with no reciprocal Palestinian property-return right.

label: Mukhmas
sub: West Bank village northeast of Jerusalem. Palestinian-American teenager Nasrallah Abu Siyam shot and killed by Israeli settlers on March 27, 2026 — second Palestinian-American killed by settlers in under a year.

label: Eshtaol — Iranian Missile Strike
sub: Jerusalem-area town struck by Iranian ballistic missile on March 28, 2026. Air defense failed to intercept; 11 lightly injured; extensive property and structural damage in 150-meter radius. IAF launched investigation into defensive failure.

label: Bani Suheila — IDF Strike Zone
sub: Area east of Khan Younis, adjacent to the Gaza ceasefire buffer zone. Site of IDF drone strike on March 28, 2026 targeting Hamas-linked vehicle; 1 killed, several wounded.

label: Tiberias
sub: Northern Israeli city on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee. Targeted by Hezbollah rocket fire on March 29, 2026 as part of coordinated multi-front attack alongside Iranian missile salvos; sirens sounded across the Galilee region.

label: Dheisheh Refugee Camp
sub: Palestinian refugee camp south of Bethlehem; established 1949 for 1948 Nakba refugees. Frequent site of IDF raids. A 15-year-old Palestinian boy was shot and killed by IDF during a March 30, 2026 military raid during a 'violent riot.'

label: WHO Vehicle Attack — Southern Gaza
sub: Israeli forces fired on a clearly marked WHO humanitarian vehicle in southern Gaza on April 6, 2026, killing one person. Incident raises serious concerns under international humanitarian law regarding protection of medical and aid workers.

label: Maghazi Refugee Camp
sub: Refugee camp in central Gaza. Site of April 7, 2026 Israeli airstrikes that killed at least 10 Palestinians after an Israeli-backed militia attempted to abduct Hamas Qassam Brigade members. Clashes between the militia and camp residents preceded the strikes.

label: Ne'ot Hovav (Ramat Hovav)
sub: Chemical industrial zone 12km south of Beersheba; Israel's most concentrated hazardous chemical manufacturing area. Struck by Iranian ballistic missile on March 29, 2026, evading air defense; hazmat emergency protocols activated with area evacuation ordered.

label: Mansoura Water Filling Station
sub: The only operational truck filling point for the Mekorot water supply line serving Gaza City. Struck by Israeli fire on April 17, 2026, killing two UNICEF-contracted water truck drivers. UNICEF suspended all contractor operations following the attack. Supplies water to hundreds of thousands of Gazans.

label: Majd al-Ba'a — Settler Arson Zone
sub: Area west of Yatta, south of Hebron, where Israeli settlers from the Otniel settlement torched two Palestinian vehicles on April 17, 2026. Part of ongoing settler violence surge in southern West Bank documented by OHCHR and B'Tselem.

label: Turmus Ayya — Settler Raid
sub: Central West Bank town near Ramallah. Raided by ~20 armed and masked settlers on April 18, 2026, who torched buildings, vandalized property, and spray-painted 'Revenge' in Hebrew. IDF later raided homes in the village but made no settler arrests.

label: al-Mughayyir — Settler School Attack
sub: Palestinian village northeast of Ramallah. On April 21, 2026, Israeli settlers attacked al-Mughayyir Boys School, killing 14-year-old student Aws Hamdi al-Na'san and 32-year-old Jihad Marzouq Abu Na'im. One of the deadliest single-day settler attacks of 2026; village has been repeatedly targeted by settlers from nearby outposts.

label: West Bank IDF Raids
sub: Israeli army conducted overnight raids across the occupied West Bank on April 12, 2026, continuing sustained military operations in the territory. Source: Al Jazeera.

label: Deir Dibwan — Settler Shooting
sub: Palestinian village east of Ramallah. On April 22, 2026, Israeli settlers shot and killed one Palestinian and injured others. Israeli forces detained approximately 30 people during the incident. Occurred one day after the deadly settler attack on al-Mughayyir school (April 21), part of an intensifying wave of settler violence during Israel's Independence Day period.

label: Duma Village — Settler Raid
sub: Palestinian village in the central West Bank, 11km southeast of Nablus. Known internationally as the site where the Dawabsheh family was burned alive by Israeli settlers in July 2015 (18-month-old baby killed). On April 25, 2026, settlers raided the village and an adjacent Bedouin hamlet, injuring a woman and a child in the latest wave of settler violence following the April 21–23 killings.

label: Sheikh Radwan — Election Day Strike Zone
sub: Neighborhood in western Gaza City. On April 25, 2026, Israeli forces struck near a Hamas police station in Sheikh Radwan while Hamas officers were deployed for election-day security during Gaza's first municipal elections in 20 years. A 14-year-old teenager also died on April 26 from injuries sustained in an earlier strike in this neighborhood.
